cases.title: Misappropriation of $4.2M in emergency food aid — South Sudan
cases.summary: World Bank INT investigation confirmed that senior management diverted emergency food assistance funds intended for internally displaced persons in South Sudan into personal accounts and affiliated shell companies. Three executives were permanently debarred from World Bank-financed projects. The fraud was uncovered through financial audit of disbursement records cross-referenced with beneficiary registers.

cases.title: Sexual exploitation of beneficiaries by field staff — DR Congo
cases.summary: UN OIOS investigation confirmed that multiple field staff members engaged in sexual exploitation of female beneficiaries during medical humanitarian operations in eastern DRC. The organization failed to act on prior internal complaints received in 2022. Two staff members were dismissed and referred to national prosecutors. The investigation found systemic failures in the organization's PSEA mechanism.

cases.title: Bid rigging in $8.7M USAID-funded shelter procurement — Yemen
cases.summary: USAID OIG audit revealed systematic bid rigging in procurement of emergency shelter materials in Yemen. The implementing partner awarded contracts exclusively to three companies with undisclosed ownership links to senior staff, at prices 40-60% above market rates. The organization failed to maintain required procurement documentation and violated mandatory competition requirements.

cases.title: Senior officials accepted bribes from construction contractors — Bangladesh
cases.summary: OCCRP investigation documented that senior officials of Hope Foundation accepted systematic payments from construction contractors in exchange for awarding development project contracts in Bangladesh. Evidence includes financial transaction records, audio recordings, witness testimonies, and electronic communications obtained through a whistleblower. Total bribe payments estimated at $1.1M over three years.

cases.title: EU development funds transferred to sanctioned entities — Syria
cases.summary: EU OLAF investigation confirmed that Levant Development Trust transferred portions of EU-funded development grants to entities on the EU consolidated sanctions list operating in Syria. The organization failed to conduct mandatory due diligence on sub-grantees and continued transfers after being notified of compliance concerns. Funds totalling €580K are subject to recovery.

cases.title: Ghost beneficiaries and falsified attendance records — Nigeria
cases.summary: FCDO annual review and independent audit found that the organization submitted systematically falsified school attendance records and ghost beneficiary lists to claim payments under a UK-funded girls education programme. An estimated 35% of reported beneficiaries were fictitious. The fraud involved collusion between field officers and local education officials.

cases.title: Invoice inflation via shell companies in WHO-funded COVID response — Papua New Guinea
cases.summary: WHO internal audit identified systematic invoice inflation in a COVID-19 health response programme in Papua New Guinea. The implementing partner procured medical supplies through shell company intermediaries linked to senior staff, at prices 200-400% above market rates. The scheme caused estimated overcharging of $930K.

cases.title: Criminal conviction for bribery in IADB-funded infrastructure — Peru
cases.summary: Lima criminal court convicted the executive director and two board members of Andean Relief Consortium for bribery in connection with an IADB-funded rural infrastructure project. The case was originally referred by the IADB Office of Institutional Integrity following a routine project audit. Convicted individuals received sentences of 4-6 years. The IADB subsequently debarred the organization.

cases.title: Double billing across USAID and EU ECHO contracts — Mali
cases.summary: Joint USAID OIG and EU OLAF investigation found that the organization systematically double-billed the same staff costs, vehicle expenses, and programme activities across simultaneous USAID and EU ECHO-funded contracts in Mali. The scheme involved duplicate invoicing and falsified timesheets. Total fraudulent claims estimated at $2.1M across both donors.

cases.title: Transactional sex by programme staff in exchange for aid distributions — Ethiopia
cases.summary: PSEA Network investigation and subsequent OIOS review confirmed that programme staff in Ethiopia conditioned food and non-food item distributions on sexual compliance by female beneficiaries at multiple distribution points. Internal reporting mechanisms had failed to surface allegations over an 18-month period. Seven staff were dismissed and criminal complaints filed with Ethiopian authorities.

cases.title: Sole-source contracting without justification — AfDB project — Kenya
cases.summary: African Development Bank integrity investigation found that Global Relief Partners repeatedly awarded sole-source contracts to a single supplier without documentation of emergency justification, as required under AfDB procurement rules. The supplier was subsequently found to be affiliated with the country director. Contracts totalling $1.8M are under review for irregularities.

cases.title: Kickback scheme with government counterparts in UNDP project — Lebanon
cases.summary: UNDP internal audit and subsequent OIOS investigation confirmed that Levant Development Trust officials paid kickbacks to Lebanese government ministry officials in exchange for project approvals and favorable inspection outcomes. The kickback rate was approximately 8% of total contract value. Senior UNDP management was notified of the scheme through an internal complaint but failed to act.

cases.title: Misrepresentation of qualifications and false certifications — GIZ project — Pakistan
cases.summary: GIZ audit found that South Asia Relief Network submitted falsified staff CVs and professional certifications to qualify for a technical assistance grant in Pakistan. None of the four listed senior technical advisors held the qualifications claimed. The organization received full payment under the grant before the fraud was detected through a third-party verification exercise.

cases.title: Staff member on UN Security Council sanctions list — Somalia operations
cases.summary: USAID compliance review discovered that a senior field coordinator employed by Horn of Africa Initiative in Somalia was listed on the UN Security Council sanctions list related to arms embargo violations. The organization failed to screen employees against sanctions lists as required by USAID standard provisions. The individual had been employed for 14 months before the discovery.

cases.title: Procurement irregularities in UNICEF-funded health supplies — South Sudan
cases.summary: UNICEF internal audit found significant procurement irregularities in a health supplies programme implemented by MedAid International in South Sudan. Irregularities included split purchase orders to avoid competitive bidding thresholds, missing evaluation documentation, and delivery of substandard medicines. An estimated 40% of delivered medicines failed quality testing.

cases.title: Criminal fraud prosecution for falsified EU audit reports — Belgium
cases.summary: Brussels criminal court indicted three Hope Foundation executives for submitting falsified financial audit reports to the European Commission for multiple development projects. Prosecution established that the organization fabricated audit trails and forged external auditor signatures on reports submitted over a five-year period. Total fraudulent claims exceed €4.8M.

cases.title: Aid diversion to armed group affiliates — Northeast Syria
cases.summary: The Sentry and UN Panel of Experts documented evidence that Crisis Response Network programme materials and food commodities in northeast Syria were systematically diverted to affiliates of an armed faction. The diversion involved collusion between NGO logistics staff and armed group gatekeepers. An estimated 25-30% of programme materials were diverted over a six-month period.
cases.source: The Sentry / UN Panel of Experts

cases.title: Bribery of government education officials to secure programme approvals — Tanzania
cases.summary: Finance Uncovered and The Guardian investigation documented that African Education Initiative paid approximately $340K in unofficial payments to Tanzanian Ministry of Education officials to secure programme implementation approvals, pass inspections, and avoid audits over a four-year period. The payments were recorded in internal accounts as 'facilitation fees'.

cases.title: Inflated staff costs and fictional consultants — World Bank project — Bolivia
cases.summary: World Bank INT investigation found that Andean Relief Consortium inflated staff costs and billed for multiple fictional consultants on a World Bank-funded rural livelihoods project in Bolivia. Staff timesheets were fabricated and consultant contracts were issued to individuals who provided no services. The organization was conditionally debarred for 18 months.

cases.title: PSEA investigation — abuse by staff during refugee camp operations — Uganda
cases.summary: UNHCR-commissioned investigation found credible evidence that Global Relief Partners staff sexually abused and exploited refugees at two camps in Uganda. The investigation identified failures in the organization's code of conduct enforcement, inadequate background checks, and suppression of prior complaints. Four staff were dismissed; the organization was placed on enhanced monitoring.

cases.title: Conflict of interest in DFAT-funded procurement — Philippines
cases.summary: Australian DFAT audit identified a significant conflict of interest in procurement processes for a livelihood programme in the Philippines. The Hope Foundation procurement officer was found to be a beneficial owner of the primary supplier, a relationship that was not disclosed. Contracts totalling AUD 1.2M were awarded without competitive process.

cases.title: Fabricated distribution records in WFP-funded operation — Burkina Faso
cases.summary: WFP Inspector General investigation found that Sahel Food Security Programme systematically fabricated beneficiary distribution records in Burkina Faso, claiming delivery of food rations to beneficiaries who either did not exist or did not receive assistance. Spot-check verification found that approximately 28% of claimed distributions were unverifiable. The organization's partnership agreement was terminated.

cases.title: ADB-financed procurement from debarred supplier — Myanmar
cases.summary: Asian Development Bank compliance audit found that MedAid International procured medical equipment from a supplier that had been debarred by the ADB and World Bank for previous fraud. The procurement was conducted without mandatory screening against the Cross-Debarment list. The organization had received explicit guidance on screening requirements six months prior.

cases.title: Fraud and abuse of authority by country director — OIOS investigation — Kenya
cases.summary: UN OIOS investigation substantiated findings of fraud and abuse of authority against the Horn of Africa Initiative country director in Kenya. The investigation found that the director diverted programme funds for personal benefit, coerced junior staff into personal work assignments, and falsified programme reports. The case was referred to Kenyan authorities for criminal prosecution.

cases.title: School construction funds diverted to private real estate — DRC
cases.summary: Global Witness investigation found that school construction grants from multiple donors were diverted by African Education Initiative management and local partners in DRC into private real estate purchases and personal accounts. Of eight schools funded, only two were partially constructed. The investigation traced fund flows through three intermediary accounts to personal property purchases.

cases.title: Rigged procurement tender in World Bank health project — Jordan
cases.summary: World Bank INT investigation substantiated bid-rigging in a health infrastructure project in Jordan. The implementing organization provided advance access to technical specifications to a preferred bidder, enabling it to tailor its proposal. The other two bidders were subsequently found to be shell companies registered to the same address as the preferred supplier.

cases.title: Systematic overstatement of overhead costs across 12 donor contracts
cases.summary: Joint review by USAID OIG, FCDO, and ECHO found that Global Relief Partners systematically overstated shared overhead costs across 12 simultaneous donor contracts, effectively charging full overhead to each donor for the same costs. An independent cost allocation review estimated $6.3M in overbilling across the affected contracts over a three-year period.

cases.title: Criminal prosecution of field coordinator for abuse — Haiti
cases.summary: Port-au-Prince court convicted a Hope Foundation field coordinator for sexual abuse of minors during post-earthquake recovery operations in Haiti. The prosecution was triggered by a UNICEF child protection referral. Investigation revealed the organization received complaints about the individual but transferred rather than dismissed him, enabling continued abuse at a second location.

cases.title: Payment of unofficial fees to customs officials for aid shipment clearance — Bangladesh
cases.summary: Transparency International Bangladesh documented that South Asia Relief Network made systematic unofficial payments to customs officials to expedite clearance of humanitarian supplies. While some facilitation payment patterns are common in the sector, TI found evidence that the organization actively budgeted for these payments as a programme cost, in violation of donor anti-bribery requirements.

cases.title: Falsified medical training certifications in UNFPA programme — Nigeria
cases.summary: UNFPA internal audit found that MedAid International submitted falsified training completion certificates and attendance lists for a reproductive health capacity building programme in Nigeria, claiming full payment for training activities that were either not conducted or attended by far fewer participants than claimed. The organization claimed for 2,400 trained health workers; verification confirmed 680.

organizations.name: Global Relief Partners
organizations.description: International humanitarian NGO operating across 38 countries with annual turnover of approximately $120M. Primarily funded by USAID, FCDO, and ECHO.

organizations.name: MedAid International
organizations.description: UK-registered medical humanitarian organization operating in 22 countries. Primary funders include ECHO, UNICEF, UNFPA, and DFID/FCDO.

organizations.name: Crisis Response Network
organizations.description: US-based emergency response organization focusing on conflict-affected areas. Primarily USAID-funded with some UN agency partnerships.

organizations.name: Hope Foundation
organizations.description: Netherlands-based development organization with operations in 29 countries. Annual revenue approximately €85M. Funded by multiple bilateral and multilateral donors.

organizations.name: Levant Development Trust
organizations.description: Regional NGO focusing on development and recovery in the Levant region. Primarily funded by the EU and UNDP.

organizations.name: African Education Initiative
organizations.description: Kenya-based education NGO operating in 11 African countries. Funded by FCDO, USAID, and the Global Partnership for Education.

organizations.name: Pacific Health Alliance
organizations.description: Australia-based health NGO focusing on Asia-Pacific. Primary funders include DFAT, WHO, and the Global Fund.

organizations.name: Andean Relief Consortium
organizations.description: Colombia-based consortium implementing IADB and World Bank-funded development projects across Andean countries.

organizations.name: Sahel Food Security Programme
organizations.description: Senegal-based NGO operating WFP, USAID, and EU-funded food security programmes across the Sahel region.

organizations.name: Horn of Africa Initiative
organizations.description: Ethiopia-based regional NGO operating food, health, education, and livelihoods programmes across the Horn of Africa.

organizations.name: South Asia Relief Network
organizations.description: Bangladesh-based NGO implementing GIZ, SIDA, and UN-funded programmes across South Asia.
